rules.title: Validation checklist — run through these for every program you review
rules.body: When validating someone else's code, work through these steps in order. Each item can be checked off as you complete it.
checklist: Run each section independently — do not run the entire program end to end as a first step
Examine every table that is created
Pay particular attention to joins (fan-out risk), unions (column alignment, UNION vs UNION ALL), and any derived variables
Confirm that created variables and the resulting output make logical sense
Check all commented-out row counts against your own log — if they differ, investigate (sometimes counts are just stale from a re-run and may be benign, but always leave a note)

rules.title: Annotate in your copy of the program, mark with your initials
rules.body: As you validate, document questions and discrepancies so the original author can find and address them.
list: Annotate directly in your copy of the program with inline comments
Optionally also capture in a separate email or document
Mark your own editorial comments with your initials

rules.title: Comment out row counts from the log next to every key table
rules.body: Row counts from the SAS log should be commented out immediately adjacent to the code that created that table. This is a team standard that allows validators to independently check their results without re-running the full program. Not required for every intermediate table, but the more the better.
